question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user pass lowerBound=0 and upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE with no type filter to force a full table scan of the registered_node table, causing a denial-of-service through excessive database CPU and memory consumption?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/RegisteredNodeRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nativeQuery=true annotation by crafting a Pageable sort parameter with an unsanitized column name to inject arbitrary SQL ORDER BY clauses, causing SQL injection or information disclosure from the registered_node table?
